title: David Michelinie

'''David Michelinie''' (born [[May 6]], [[1940s|1948]]) is an American comic book writer who was the regular writer on the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] comic series from [[1981]] to [[1983]].

His most famous works include a noted run on the [[Wikipedia:Iron Man|''Iron Man'']] series in the early 1980s which introduced the character's serious problem with alcoholism, his specialized power armor variants and his new enmities with [[Wikipedia:Justin Hammer|Justin Hammer]] and [[Wikipedia:Doctor Doom|Victor Von Doom]]. His most noted cliffhanger was when Tony Stark was thrown out of [[Wikipedia:S.H.I.E.L.D.|S.H.I.E.L.D.'s]] helicarrier and had to don his armor completely to use its flight function before he hit the ground.

In the late 1980s, he also wrote a popular run on [[Wikipedia:The Amazing Spider-Man|''The Amazing Spider-Man'']] series which featured the art of [[Wikipedia:Todd McFarlane|Todd McFarlane]] and introduced the supervillain [[Wikipedia:Venom (comics)|Venom]]. He also has the record of having the longest run on The Amazing Spider-Man comic, a record that has still not been surpassed.

Michelinie co-created several Star Wars characters including [[Plif/Legends|Plif]], [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]], and [[Fenn Shysa]].
